model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"With only 256 fixed training steps on full CIFAR-10, the current Adam learning rate is likely too conservative and underfits early. Increasing LR is the simplest high-impact change for faster loss reduction within the same step budget.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The previous learning-rate increase produced a clear gain, suggesting the run is still in an under-tuned early-optimization regime at 256 steps. A further modest LR increase is a low-complexity follow-up likely to improve within-budget convergence.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 4e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Loss continued improving after each LR increase, so the optimization is likely still below its best early-step learning-rate regime. A small additional increase keeps the change simple while testing whether faster progress within 256 steps is still available.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 4e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The 4e-3 change regressed validation loss, indicating we passed the local optimum. Reverting to 3e-3 is the safest correction to recover the best observed learning-rate regime before testing other axes.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 0.0","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"With only 256 training steps, underfitting is more likely than overfitting; removing weight decay can improve early optimization and reduce bias from regularization before significant memorization risk appears.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 8","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"After improving optimization dynamics, the model is likely capacity-limited. A modest width increase should improve representation power while keeping architecture simple and stable under the same training budget.","secondary_modes":["topk","caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 8","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The width increase regressed validation loss under the fixed-step budget, indicating slower optimization outweighed extra capacity. Reverting to the smaller width restores the better compute-efficient regime.","secondary_modes":["topk","caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"For a short 256-step budget, a lower Adam beta2 can react faster to gradient statistics and often improves early convergence. This is a minimal optimizer-dynamics tweak on top of the current best settings.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"After improving Adam’s short-horizon dynamics (beta2=0.99), the optimal LR may shift upward. A moderate LR increase tests for additional within-budget convergence without adding architectural complexity.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 3.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The LR increase to 3.5e-3 slightly worsened loss, so returning to 3e-3 restores the stronger operating point with beta2=0.99 before exploring other dimensions.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.85, 0.99)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"Lowering beta1 can increase responsiveness of momentum in short training horizons, which may improve early-step progress at fixed budget with otherwise stable settings.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.85, 0.99)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"The lower beta1 setting degraded validation loss, so reverting to beta1=0.9 restores the best-performing optimizer dynamics found so far.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Under a fixed 256-step budget, increasing batch size doubles the number of training examples seen and can reduce gradient noise, which may improve validation loss with minimal code complexity.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The large improvement from batch 128 suggests this fixed-step setting benefits from processing more examples per step. Testing batch 256 is the most direct continuation to potentially improve further.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Batch 256 regressed relative to the current best, so reverting to batch 128 restores the stronger tradeoff between sample throughput and optimization quality.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 4e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"With batch size increased to 128, a higher learning rate may better match the lower gradient noise and improve progress per step. This tests a simple large-batch LR scaling from the current best configuration.","secondary_modes":["micro","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 4e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The LR increase to 4e-3 slightly worsened loss at batch 128, so reverting to 3e-3 restores the current best-performing operating point.","secondary_modes":["micro","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 32","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"With optimization now strong (batch 128, tuned Adam), the small classifier head may be the next bottleneck. Increasing the hidden size is a minimal capacity change focused on improving class separation.","secondary_modes":["micro","indexing"]}
